settings.merge_by_version_check: Separate output by script API version (merge same versions together; recommended to avoid script conflicts)
settings.customize_pack_after_merge_tool: After merge completes, prompt to set pack name, description, and icon.
settings.customize_pack_after_merge_chec: Prompt to name pack and pick icon after merge
settings.merge_by_version_tooltip: When enabled, Start Process groups packs by their script API version (@minecraft/server etc.) and merges same versions together. Each version group is merged separately: one behavior_pack + resource_pack per script version, with each BP matched to its correct RP. Output folders are named by version (e.g. 1_8_0/, 2_5_0_beta/, none/). Use Check Packs to see which versions are safe to merge.

help.overview: Overview – What AutoBE Is & How to Use It
help.overview_what_is: AutoBE is a desktop tool for Minecraft Bedrock addons. It lets you merge multiple addons into one pack, process or repack .mcpack/.mcaddon files, and create lists of packs. Use the tabs at the top to switch between tools.
help.overview_tabs_autobe: • AutoBE tab – Merge addons: Add .mcpack files, choose an output folder, then click Start Process. You get one resource_pack and one behavior_pack. Use Check Packs to see each addon's script API version; only merge addons that use the same script API (or turn on "Merge by script version" in Settings to merge per script version automatically).
help.overview_tabs_mcpacker: • MCPACKER tab – In Settings choose Pack (folders → .mcpack) or Extract (.mcpack/.mcaddon → folders). Add files, pick an output folder, then Start.
help.overview_tabs_list_maker: • List Maker tab – Build lists: Add .mcpack files to "Merged List" or "Alone List", then Export List to save a text list of pack names. Use this to organize or document which packs you use.
help.overview_activation: You need an internet connection the first time you use AutoBE. Enter your activation key (or have your device whitelisted). Once activated, your device is remembered and you can use AutoBE offline. If you see "Connect to the internet to verify", connect to the internet and open AutoBE again so it can verify; after that you can go offline again.
help.common_errors_full: Common Errors & How to Avoid Them
help.welcome_text: AutoBE tab: Add .mcpack or .mcaddon files, choose an output folder, then Start Process. Use Check Packs to see script API versions—only merge packs that use the same script API, or use "Merge by script version" in Settings. MCPACKER: set Pack or Extract in Settings, add files, pick output, then Start. List Maker: choose Merged or Alone list, add files, then Export List.

compatibility.title: Compatibility Check
msg.no_mcpack_added: No MCPACK files have been added.
addons_used.title: The Addons Used For This Merging
help.usage1_desc: On the AutoBE tab, click Add Files and select one or more .mcpack or .mcaddon files. They appear in the list. You can remove any with Remove Selected.
help.usage2_desc: Click Browse next to the output directory and pick where to save. AutoBE will create resource_pack and behavior_pack there (and, if you use "Merge by script version" in Settings, one folder per version).
help.usage3_desc: Click Check Packs to see each addon's script API version (the scripting dependency, e.g. @minecraft/server). Only merge packs that use the same script API—mixing different script versions breaks scripts. If you have mixed versions, turn on "Merge by script version" in Settings so AutoBE merges each script version separately.
help.usage4_desc: Click Start Process. If an addon has multiple subpacks, a window will ask you to pick one. Wait until the progress bar finishes; the list clears when done.
help.usage5_desc: Import both resource_pack and behavior_pack into your world and test. If something breaks, remove the addon that causes it and run the merge again without that pack.
help.merge1_desc: AutoBE creates a resource_pack and a behavior_pack. Both are needed in Minecraft. The resource pack has textures, sounds, and UI; the behavior pack has entities, items, scripts, and game logic.
help.merge2_desc: When addons conflict (same IDs or same files), AutoBE renames and merges where it can. Some addons that change the same things in different ways may still conflict—always test the merged pack.
help.merge3_desc: For addons that use scripts: they depend on a script API version (e.g. 1.8.0, 2.5.0). Packs with different script API versions must not be merged together or scripts break. Use Check Packs to see each pack's script API version. In Settings, "Merge by script version" groups packs by that version and merges each group separately, so you get one merge per script version.
help.error1_desc: The file isn't a valid addon or is damaged. Use .mcpack or .mcaddon files, try opening them in Minecraft first, or re-download the addon.
help.error2_desc: Mixing packs that use different script API versions breaks scripts. Use Check Packs to see each pack's script API version; only merge packs that use the same one. Or turn on "Merge by script version" in Settings so AutoBE outputs one merge per script version.
help.error3_title: Connect to the internet to verify
help.error3_desc: Your device isn't verified yet. Connect to the internet and open AutoBE again so it can verify. After that you can use it offline.
help.error4_desc: Check that the output folder is writable and you have enough disk space. Try a different folder or run as administrator if you get permission errors.
help.error5_desc: Some addons have multiple subpacks. A window will open—pick the subpack you want to include. Only one subpack per addon is used.
help.practice1: Test addons one by one before merging so you know they work.
help.practice2: Use Check Packs to see script API versions; only merge packs that use the same script API.
help.practice3: Keep backups of your original addon files.
help.practice4: Test the merged pack in Minecraft right after creating it.
help.practice5: If something breaks, remove one addon at a time and re-merge to find the one causing the issue.
help.practice6: Respect addon creators' terms and only use addons you're allowed to merge.
help.proc1_desc: AutoBE reads your packs and builds one combined manifest with version and dependency info.
help.proc2_desc: Files are extracted and split into resource pack and behavior pack content.
help.proc3_desc: Content is merged, conflicts are fixed where possible, and references are updated.
help.proc4_desc: The final resource_pack and behavior_pack are written to your output folder.
help.disc1_desc: Test addons individually first, then test the merged pack in Minecraft. AutoBE fixes technical conflicts but can't fix addons that clash by design.
help.disc2_desc: Packs that use different script API versions should not be merged together—scripts will break. Use Check Packs to see script versions. Use "Merge by script version" in Settings to get one merged pack per script version.
help.disc3_desc: For help: thebedrocklabhelp@gmail.com or join our Discord (TheCodeNex). Owners: FrostyHostMC, Eldas.
help.disc4_desc: Refunds, bans, license terms, and other legal info are in the Terms of Service you accepted when you first opened AutoBE.
